content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining run-lifecycle hardening in runtime code. `src/adapters/fs.rs` now persists active backend process-group leaders under `runtime/backend/active-processes.json`, exposes read/write/liveness helpers for those records, and reuses the same process-identity checks for both `run.pid` and backend-process tracking. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` now registers each spawned backend child against the live project before invocation proceeds and removes that record once teardown is confirmed. In `src/cli/run.rs`, stale `run stop` and stale `run resume` now kill any persisted backend process groups for the exact stale run attempt before reconciling `run.json`, preventing resumed overlap with orphaned backend workers after orchestrator death. The legacy pid-only fallback was also tightened so a live non-authoritative `run.pid` only blocks recovery when a fresh matching lease still exists; otherwise `run status` reports stale and `run stop`/`run resume` recover instead of wedging behind a reused unrelated PID. Added regression coverage in `tests/cli.rs` plus adapter unit coverage for backend-process tracking, stale stop/resume backend cleanup, and the new legacy-pid stale behavior without a matching lease.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Extended filesystem lifecycle state to persist active backend process leaders alongside `run.pid`, including project-root helpers for registering, reading, pruning, and liveness-checking persisted backend process records.
2. [Completed] Updated the process backend adapter to register spawned backend children in the persisted backend-process file before execution continues and to remove those records once child teardown is confirmed.
3. [Completed] Patched CLI run lifecycle handling so stale `run stop` and stale `run resume` kill persisted backend process groups for the exact stale run attempt before reconciling snapshot state, and tightened legacy pid-only liveness so raw live PIDs require a fresh matching lease before recovery is blocked.
4. [Completed] Added regressions for backend-process tracking, stale stop/resume backend cleanup, and legacy pid-only stale recovery without a matching lease, then reran formatting, focused validations, the full test suite, and clippy.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_status_reports_stale_for_live_legacy_pid_without_proc_start_ticks_when_no_lease_exists -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_stop_recovers_live_legacy_pid_record_without_proc_start_ticks_when_no_lease_exists -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_stop_recovers_stale_running_and_kills_persisted_backend_process_group -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli run_resume_recovers_live_legacy_pid_record_without_proc_start_ticks_when_no_lease_exists -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_resume_kills_persisted_stale_backend_process_group_before_restarting -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` failed only on pre-existing repository-wide lint findings outside this lifecycle patch set.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing repository-wide lint debt outside this lifecycle patch, with current findings in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`, `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs`, `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs`.
- The stale backend-process-group cleanup path is covered in this Linux/Unix validation environment, but there is still no dedicated non-Unix end-to-end harness for the platform-specific fallback behavior.
- The worktree already had unrelated live-state changes under `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/`; those files were left untouched.
